title: Build a Dockerfile as rootless not possible via Gitlab
body:
I try to build an Image from Docker file via Gitlab like this:
`deploy:
  stage: deploy
  script:
    - echo "the project directory is - $CI_PROJECT_DIR"
    - cat $CI_PROJECT_DIR/README.md ; echo
    - echo "and where am I? - $PWD"
    - echo "CONTAINER_OWNER=$(whoami)"
    - echo "Start building image for client"
    - cd client
    - podman build -t lm-client-dev .
    - echo "Start creating container"
    - podman run lm-client-dev -d --restart=always --pod=lm-dev`
  but I get error as follow: 
`$ podman build -t lm-client-dev .
time="2021-07-30T14:25:56+02:00" level=error msg="cannot find mappings for user vagrant: No subuid ranges found for user \"vagrant\" in /etc/subuid"
STEP 1: FROM node:16.5-alpine3.14
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:e9d0d9a61f19bb1a72fa8e0ba3b59c540b5fb3efdcfd18ef791c89df65124ac5
Copying blob sha256:5843afab387455b37944e709ee8c78d7520df80f8d01cf7f861aae63beeddb6b
Copying blob sha256:a323d77b1d49df4d6e599429af7f9d22261517634f01b40c9e5d667605a6bbde

Copying config sha256:f88956284d230b0e2a76638c1be616e272b5b9e7db19c41bacd356a857154bb2
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
Error: error creating build container: The following failures happened while trying to pull image specified by "node:16.5-alpine3.14" based on search registries in /etc/containers/registries.conf:
* "localhost/node:16.5-alpine3.14": Error initializing source docker://localhost/node:16.5-alpine3.14: error pinging docker registry localhost: Get https://localhost/v2/: dial tcp [::1]:443: connect: connection refused
* "registry.access.redhat.com/node:16.5-alpine3.14": Error initializing source docker://registry.access.redhat.com/node:16.5-alpine3.14: Error reading manifest 16.5-alpine3.14 in registry.access.redhat.com/node: name unknown: Repo not found
* "registry.redhat.io/node:16.5-alpine3.14": Error initializing source docker://registry.redhat.io/node:16.5-alpine3.14: unable to retrieve auth token: invalid username/password: unauthorized: Please login to the Red Hat Registry using your Customer Portal credentials. Further instructions can be found here: https://access.redhat.com/RegistryAuthentication
* "docker.io/library/node:16.5-alpine3.14": Error committing the finished image: error adding layer with blob "sha256:5843afab387455b37944e709ee8c78d7520df80f8d01cf7f861aae63beeddb6b": Error processing tar file(exit status 1): there might not be enough IDs available in the namespace (requested 0:42 for /etc/shadow): lchown /etc/shadow: invalid argument`

On my host (redhat 8) I have registered my auth token and have added a user named vagrant:
grep vagrant /etc/subuid /etc/subgid
/etc/subuid:vagrant:1083040:65536
/etc/subuid:vagrant:200000:1001

but still getting the same error.

comments.body: Can you provide the Dockerfile that Podman is being instructed to build? Really need to see the `FROM` line.

sure:
`FROM node:16.5-alpine3.14

WORKDIR /usr/src/app

COPY package*.json /usr/src/app/package.json
RUN npm install

COPY . /usr/src/app

EXPOSE 3000

CMD ["npm", "run", "start"]`

```
idMappings:
gidmap: null
uidmap: null
```

From your `podman info` - looks like UID and GID mappings are not set up.

Please try running `podman system migrate`

Thanks a lot for your answer. The problem was that the runner for GitLab is running in a VM which I was not aware of. So the changes I made on the server had no effect on that. I added the uid in VM and it works.
